sections.title: DEFINITION OF PRODUCE
sections.text: This Standard applies to commercial varieties of Mexican limes grown from Citrus aurantifolia Swingle, of the Rutaceae family, to be supplied fresh to the consumer, after preparation and packaging. Mexican limes for industrial processing are excluded.
sections.retrieval_text:
DEFINITION OF PRODUCE
number=1
level=1
This Standard applies to commercial varieties of Mexican limes grown from Citrus aurantifolia Swingle, of the Rutaceae family, to be supplied fresh to the consumer, after preparation and packaging. Mexican limes for industrial processing are excluded.

page_spans.text: This Standard applies to commercial varieties of Mexican limes grown from Citrus aurantifolia Swingle, of the Rutaceae family, to be supplied fresh to the consumer, after preparation and packaging. Mexican limes for industrial processing are excluded.

sections.title: The Mexican limes must have reached an appropriate degree of development and ripeness in accordance with criteria proper to the variety and to the area in which they are grown.
path_titles: The Mexican limes must have reached an appropriate degree of development and ripeness in accordance with criteria proper to the variety and to the area in which they are grown.
sections.text:
The development and condition of the Mexican limes must be such as to enable them:  
­ to withstand transport and handling; and 
­ to arrive in satisfactory condition at the place of destination.

sections.title: Minimum Juice Content and Colouring
sections.text:
The minimum juice content is calculated in relation to the total weight of the fruit. 
- Minimum juice content: 40% 
Colouring must be typical of the variety on at least two-thirds of the fruit surface. The fruit should be green but may show discolouring (yellow patches) up to 30% of its surface.

page_spans.text:
The minimum juice content is calculated in relation to the total weight of the fruit. 
- Minimum juice content: 40% 
Colouring must be typical of the variety on at least two-thirds of the fruit surface. The fruit should be green but may show discolouring (yellow patches) up to 30% of its surface.

sections.title: "Extra" Class
sections.text: Mexican limes in this class must be of superior quality. They must be characteristic of the variety. They must be free of defects, with the exception of very slight superficial defects, provided these do not affect the general appearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package.

page_spans.text: Mexican limes in this class must be of superior quality. They must be characteristic of the variety. They must be free of defects, with the exception of very slight superficial defects, provided these do not affect the general appearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package.

sections.title: Class I
sections.retrieval_text:
PROVISIONS CONCERNING QUALITY > Classification > Class I
number=2.2.2
level=3
Mexican limes in this class must be of good quality. They must be characteristic of the variety. The following slight defects, however, may be allowed, provided these do not affect the general appearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package: 
­ slight defects in shape; 
­ slight defects in colouring; 
­ slight skin defects not exceeding more than 1 cm2. 
The defects must not, in any case, affect the pulp of the fruit.

sections.title: PROVISIONS CONCERNING SIZING
sections.text:
Size is determined by the maximum diameter of the equatorial section of the fruit, in accordance with the following table:
Size Code Diameter (mm)
1 > 45
2 40.1 - 45
3 35.1 - 40
4 30.1 - 35
5 25 - 30

sections.title: PROVISIONS CONCERNING TOLERANCES
sections.text: Tolerances in respect of quality and size shall be allowed in each package (or in each lot for produce presented in bulk) for produce not satisfying the requirements of the class indicated.

level=1

sections.title: Size Tolerances
sections.text: For all classes, 10% by number or weight of Mexican limes corresponding to the size  immediately above or below that indicated on the package.

sections.title: Uniformity
sections.text: The contents of each package (or lot for produce presented in bulk) must be uniform and contain only Mexican limes of the same origin, variety, quality and size. For “Extra” Class, the colour must be uniform. The visible part of the contents of the package (or lot for produce presented in bulk) must be representative of the entire contents.

sections.title: Packaging
sections.text: Mexican limes must be packed in such a way as to protect the produce properly. The materials used inside the package must be new 1, clean, and of a quality such as to avoid causing any external or internal damage

sections.title: Class II
sections.text: This class includes Mexican limes which do not qualify for inclusion in the higher classes, but satisfy the minimum requirements specified in Section 2.1 above. The following defects, however, may be allowed, provided the Mexican limes retain their essential characteristics as regards the quality, the keeping quality and presentation: defects in shape; defects in colouring; defects in the skin due to scratches, scabs, abrasions, spots or other skin defects not exceeding more than 2 cm2. The defects must not, in any case, affect the pulp of the fruit.

sections.title: MARKING OR LABELLING
sections.text: In addition to the requirements of the General Standard for the Labelling of Prepackaged Foods (CXS 1-1985), the following specific provisions apply:
sections.retrieval_text:
MARKING OR LABELLING
number=6
level=1
In addition to the requirements of the General Standard for the Labelling of Prepackaged Foods (CXS 1-1985), the following specific provisions apply:

page_spans.text: In addition to the requirements of the General Standard for the Labelling of Prepackaged Foods (CXS 1-1985), the following specific provisions apply:

sections.title: CONTAMINANTS
sections.text: The produce covered by this Standard shall comply with the maximum levels of the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995). The produce covered by this Standard shall comply with the maximum residue limits for pesticides established by the Codex Alimentarius Commission.
sections.retrieval_text:
CONTAMINANTS
number=7
level=1
The produce covered by this Standard shall comply with the maximum levels of the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995). The produce covered by this Standard shall comply with the maximum residue limits for pesticides established by the Codex Alimentarius Commission.

page_spans.text: The produce covered by this Standard shall comply with the maximum levels of the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995). The produce covered by this Standard shall comply with the maximum residue limits for pesticides established by the Codex Alimentarius Commission.

sections.title: HYGIENE
sections.text: It is recommended that the produce covered by the provisions of this Standard be prepared and handled in accordance with the appropriate sections of the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969), Code of Hygienic Practice for Fresh Fruits and Vegetables (CXC 53-2003), and other relevant Codex texts such as Codes of Hygienic Practice and Codes of Practice. The produce should comply with any microbiological criteria established in accordance with the Principles and Guidelines for the Establishment and Application of Microbiological Criteria Related to Foods (CXG 21-1997).

The produce should comply with any microbiological criteria established in accordance with the Principles and Guidelines for the Establishment and Application of Microbiological Criteria Related to Foods (CXG 21-1997).

page_spans.text: It is recommended that the produce covered by the provisions of this Standard be prepared and handled in accordance with the appropriate sections of the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969), Code of Hygienic Practice for Fresh Fruits and Vegetables (CXC 53-2003), and other relevant Codex texts such as Codes of Hygienic Practice and Codes of Practice. The produce should comply with any microbiological criteria established in accordance with the Principles and Guidelines for the Establishment and Application of Microbiological Criteria Related to Foods (CXG 21-1997).

sections.title: Non-retail Containers

sentences.original: It is recommended that the produce covered by the provisions of this Standard be prepared and handled in accordance with the appropriate sections of the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969), Code of Hygienic Practice for Fresh Fruits and Vegetables (CXC 53-2003), and other relevant Codex texts such as Codes of Hygienic Practice and Codes of Practice.

pages.title: STANDARD FOR MEXICAN LIMES
pages.summary: Página inicial do documento contendo apenas título principal e cabeçalho. Sem seções ou texto de conteúdo para mapear. Valor para RAG conservador devido à ausência de estrutura textual.

Página contém definições iniciais e provisões sobre qualidade dos limões mexicanos com hierarquia clara de títulos numerados até nível 3. Continuações indicadas para seções que seguem na próxima página.
Página com títulos estruturados numerados até nível 3 e títulos em caixa alta para níveis 1 e 2. Texto associado claramente a cada seção. Continuidade indicativa nas seções 2.2.3 e 5.2.

Página contém títulos estruturados numerados dos níveis 1 a 3, com texto associado detalhado e clara associação hierárquica, adequada para RAG. A seção 6 apresenta subseções detalhadas. A seção 6 aparece como continuação da página anterior e parece continuar na próxima página.
